remedies.condition: Abscesses, skin: An abscess often results from an embedded foreign object or from an underlying infection.  The body forms a cavity or pocket around the area to wall off the infection and protect the rest of the body.  A pocket of pus forms around the area as the body attempts to push out the foreign object or fight off the infection     Choose one and give e15 minutes.     Decrease with improvement.    Symptoms include localized heat, redness, pain and swelling.
remedies: Aconite 30c, - For extreme redness and shining skin around the abscess or a fever with hot dry skin. Symptoms are aggravated by a warm evening.
Belladonna 30D - For redness and throbbing pain with a lack of pus.
Bryonia alba 30c, - Use when the infected area is shiny and red with stitching pain. Movement makes the pain worse.
Calcarea Carb 30c, - Use when the infected area is pale, not red.
Calcarea sulph 30c,  - For slow healing after rupture of the abscess, with pus that continues to discharge. The person desires open air but is sensitive to drafts.
Calendula 30c, - Use when the abscess secretes thick yellow pus with no inflammation.
Chamomilla 30c, - Use when the abscess is accompanied by unbearable pain.
Echinacea 30c, - For any infection, especially when sepsis is present. It supports the immune system when fighting an infection.
Fluoric acid 30c, - Especially useful after administering Silicea
Hepar sulph 30c, - This remedy can stop formation of an abscess if administered in the early stages. Indicators include thick yellow pus oozing from the site. Symptoms become worse in drafts.
Lachesis 30c, - For thin, dark, bluish pus with a putrid smelling discharge, sensitive to touch, with burning pain.
Lycopodium 30c, - For abscesses under the skin. Symptoms are worse with warmth.
Merc sol 30c, - For rapid formation of an abscess, especially helpful for glandular areas. Symptoms are aggravated by a warm bed.
Pulsatilla 30c, - For an abscess with a large amount of yellow, green or bloody pus. Symptoms are aggravated by rich food and warmth. Cold, open air is helpful.
Myrstica sebifera 30c, - Especially good for abscesses on the fingertips.

Nitric acid 30c, - For abscesses around the glands (particularly those in the groin or underarm). The pus smells bad and is a thick greenish-yellow color. Contact with pus breaks down the skin around the abscess.
Pyrogenium 30c, - For recurring abscesses that have pus with no drainage.

Rhus tox 30c, - This remedy is helpful for acute, painful abscesses especially around the eye or ear. The abscess is bright red and swollen and leaks pus with blood and lymph mixed together. This is the premiere remedy for septicemia.
Silicea 30c, - For thin, watery pus and chilliness. The symptoms become better in dry warmth and worse in cold/wetness.
Stramonium 30c, - For extreme pain in the joints, especially the left hip.
Sulphur 30c, - For chronic abscesses with profuse discharge of pus, especially if the person is feverish. The pus smells bad and causes any skin it touches to break down.

remedies.condition: Allergic Reaction, Anaphylactic Shock, Allergies in General
remedies: Arsenicum Album 30c, e10min - 30min SOS

Aconitum napellis 200X - This remedy is the most effective within the first hour of a critical incident. It addresses shock with anxious agitation and an overwhelming fear of death. It is also used for cutting, stabbing or inflammatory burning pains with swlling, tingling or numbness. The symptoms are better in fresh air and worse in a warm room or at night. This person does not want to be touched. Combine with Kali Phos 6X and Nat Sulph 6X, one dose of each, every 30 minutes and less frequently as signs of improvement are seen. Use for up to 3 days.

Carbolic acid 200X- For anaphalactic shock from bee sting with pain that feels like pinpricks, swelling of face, throat and tongue and eyes swollen to the point of being swollen shut. Look for a dusky or red face with white skin around the nose and mouth. This person feels sick and weak and has difficulty breathing. This remedy is more difficult to procure, but ifeel it so important for people to have this information so I include it. If your homeopathic pharmacist does not carry this, contact Joette's office for more information.

Apis Mellifica 200c- For rapid swelling of tongue, lips, and eyelids, difficulty breathing, anaphalactic shock and allergic reactions. For red itchy hives or insect bites that feel hot and swell quickly. The person may a sore throat and swollen tonsils/ Uvula with a red, dry tongue and mouth, without thirst. Listen for a short, dry cough. In general, heat and pressure make things worse, and cold improves the symptoms. Note: Apris should never be given during pregnancy.

Arsenicum album 30c- For allergic reactions in general, particularly if the person is very sensitive to smells. This person feels weak (can be to teh point of collapse) and agitated at the same time. They may be exhausted but unable to sleep. Unlike Apis, the symptoms are better from warmth and elevation of the head and worse from the cold

remedies.condition: Allergic Reaction, Anaphalactic Shock - continued   The remedies to the right may also be considered to address allergy symptoms that are not accompanied by anaphylactic shock. Use 30c potency and adminsiter one dose every 15 minutes, reducing frequency depending on severity of symptoms. Stop when improvement is seen.
remedies: Ledum 30c- For puncture wounds or bites, especially if they feel cold, but a cold compress brings improvement. The wound is painful to touch with a cold mottled blue swelling around it. The person feels cold overall, with numbness and aching. The symptoms improve with the application of cold and are worse at night or from heat. (Can also be used in the 200c potency in emergency situations)

Carbo veg 30c- For the person who is unconcious or has fainted or after collapse from anaphylactic shock. The person may be short of breath or have seriously depressed respiration and bluish skin. The person has a hot head but icy cold extremities. Carbo veg has a reputation for turning around even the most serious cases of collapse. It is also helpful for any type of breathing difficulty. Symptoms improve from fresh air. If the person is unconscious, give one dose of Carbo veg 30c in aqueous solution on the inside of the wrist every 15 minutes.

Opium 30c- For the person who is unconscious or semi-unconscious with dark face and open mouth (perhaps twitching at the corners), pupils constricted and unresponsive, eyelids half closed and eyes bloodshot. If the person is unconcious, administer one dose of Opium 30c in the aqueous solution on teh inside of the wrist every 15 minutes.

Camphora 30c- For the person who is unconscious, or semi-conscious or very weak as a result of shock or injury, with weak pulse and respiration and cold skin. The symptoms are worse from cold, but the person resists being covered. If the person is unconscious, give one dose of camphora 30c in aqueous solution on the inside of the wrist every 15 minutes.

Phosphorus 30c- For shortness of breath, lung congestion and asthma made worse by humid weather, gasping and panting for air with a sensation of chest constriction, hypersensitivity to and coughing from odors and fumes, headache with visual disturbances and vertigo. Symptoms improve from drinking cold water.

Cantharis 30c- For severe skin reaction with redness and swlling, intense burning pain and inflammation. The area feels scalded and blisters may develop. Symptoms improve from rubbing the affected area.
Hypericum 30c -For bites and stings (puncture wounds) with shooting pains, numbness and tingling in the affected area.

remedies.condition: The remedies to the right may also be considered to address allergy symptoms that are not accompanied by anaphylactic shock. Use 30c potency and adminsiter one dose every 15 minutes, reducing frequency depending on severity of symptoms. Stop when improvement is seen.
remedies: Urtica urens 30c- For red, burning, intensely itchy blotches or hives that feel like stinging nettles or like ants crawling under the skin, allergic reactions to shellfish, vertigo and headache. Symptoms are worse from cool moist air and touch.

Kali bichromium 30c- For pressure in teh sinuses, heacache over the eyebrows; throat and lung inflammation with congestion and cough and shortness of breath with greenish, thick, stringy secretions. Symptoms are worse in the morning.

Hepar sulph 30c- For sinus pain and inflammation with breath smelling like spoiled milk, raw sore throat with airway inflammation, croupy cough with thick secretions, hypersensitivity to touch, pain and cold air. The person feels better from warm, moist air.
Allumium cepa 30c- For a hoarse, spasmodic, painful cough coming from the larynx, profuse watery discharge from the nose, teary eyes and repetitive violent sneezing.

Rhus toxicondendron 30c-- For itchy, inflamed (perhaps blistered) skin with a crawling sensation, restlessness, inability to settle down, chilliness and stiff, sore joints. Symptoms are better from motion and warmth.

Mormon Tea (Ephedra viridis) is a plant that contains natural ephedrine. It can be useful in controlling bronchitis and asthma symptoms. For those who like to be prepared for potential long-term disaster scenarios, it would be prudent to grow this shrub at home and use it to make a tincture for storage or freshly made strong tea in an emergency if no tincture is at hand.

remedies.condition: Amputation or Avulsion (a flap of skin torn loose)
remedies: Clean wound with Calendula or Hypericum tincture (diluted 1:10-1tsp per quart of water). If a flap of skin is loose or there is a partial amputation, lay the part back in place and cover or wrap it with a clean dry dressing.  If the amputation is fully, wrap the amputated part in a dry sterile dressing, place it in a ziplock pack and sprinkle it with just a few drops of water.  Keep this cool while transporting, but do not place it in direct contact with ice.  Apply direct pressure to the wound to slow down bleeding.

remedies.condition: Anger - When anger is out of control, out of proportion or irrational, when it persists long after a causative event or when its suppression leads to physical complaints, homeopaths recommend:
remedies: Staphysagria 30c, For deep anger and indignation following abuse or violent attack, feeling unable to express pain over perceived insults, resentment and blaming after a loss, trembling, insomnia, easily angered but doesn’t often show it, crying and feeling sorry for oneself.  This person hates conflict.

Chamomilla 30c, For contrariness and fractiousness; dissatisfaction, cannot be pleased, quarrelsome, hypersensitivity and intolerance of pain, diarrhea, cough, loses temper over insignificant matters, perspiration and one cheek red while the other is pale.

Nux Vomica 30c, For the person who is irritable, impatient, intolerant, critical and argumentative, irritated by noise and other external stimuli, experiences anger from fright and is competitive and blames others.  This person feels worse from stimulants.

Aurum metallicum 30c, For deep despair, self-reproach, feeling like a failure, high standards and idealism, alternating between tortured silence and great anger.  Any contradiction results in anger.  This person suppresses their angry feelings and does not often lose control and tends to turn to meditation or prayer.

Nitric acid 30c, For outbursts of extreme anger, hatred and fantasies of revenge, insomnia with exhaustion, gnawing anger that leads to depression and hopeless despair, sensitivity to touch, pain or noise, negative attitude and splinter-like pains.

Anacardium 30c, For anger, abusive language, moral conflict, violent rages, two personalities, like Jekyll and Hyde, alternating moods from joy and sadness, cruelty and maliciousness combined with extreme and widespread fears, feelings of inferiority, great anxiety, lack of sensitivity to physical injury and numbness.  The person feels better from eating, cannot remember the word he wants, feels possessed or under the control of someone else and is indecisive.

Sepia 30c, For the person who is irritable, weary, worn out, indifferent to loved ones, wants to be left alone and experiences slow digestion and angry outbursts.  The person feels better from vigorous exercise and crying, experiences a bearing-down sensation in the abdomen and does not want to be consoled or cheered up.

Aconitum 30c, For anger from shock or fright, intense anger where the person feels as though she wants to kill her loved ones, intolerance of contradiction, irritated by family, feels that everything they do is wrong, impatience, anxiety over loved ones and immediate overreaction to bad news.  This person expresses feelings easily but does not want consolation.

Belladonna 30c, For the person who becomes restive when ill, with sudden destructive rage and an urge to bite others in anger.  Other symptoms include high fever, glassy eyes, intensity, irritability, impatience, outbursts of temper, anger with shouting, coughing and red face, desire to break things, anger alternating with weeping, mania and delusions. This person reacts explosively to advice when angry, but the anger subsides quickly like a brief summer thunderstorm.
Ignatia 30c, For suppressed anger with trembling, hysteria and changing moods with alternating between opposites and sighing.  This person suffers from headache after expressing anger.
Petroleum 30c, For the person who is irritable, quick to anger and argumentative.  Other highlights include travel sickness and eczema.

Colocynthis 30c, For great indignation, anger when questioned or contradicted, constant complaining, irritability, impatience, vomiting, diarrhea or colic, neuralgia, abdominal pain after anger, relieved by pressure, illness from pent-up unexpressed anger, restlessness and weakness.
Antimonium crudum 30c, For peevishness, excitability, acne and poor digestion.  The person cannot bear to be looked at or touched and is easily angered.
Arnica montana 30c, For anger that follows a fright or trauma.

Stramonium 30c, For symptoms that come on after a fright, raging out-of-control anger, delusions, loudness and loquaciousness and violence towards others due to fear.  The person feels better from light and company.

Bryonia 30c, For the person who is highly irritable and wants to be left alone.  Symptoms include digestive problems, respiratory problems or headaches after an angry outburst, dizziness from sitting up, dry parched mouth, anxiety, anger when frustrated and red face.

Mercurius solubilis 30c, For sudden violent anger, sensitivity to temperature changes, violent impulses with fears that he will act on those impulses, hurried hastiness, restlessness, sweaty head, fear resulting in aggression, bleeding gums, sneezing in sunlight, suspicious and mistrustful.  The person feels worse at night.

Hepar Sulph 30c, For the person who is discontented and irritated by noise, pain, cold drafts and slight annoyances.  Other symptoms include violent temper tantrums, impulsiveness, obstinacy, anxiety, fearfulness, a trial to others, aversion to company and estrangement from loved ones despite intense care and worry for them, acting out against those loved ones, violent, poor memory, hurried, speaks and eats quickly, destructive, wants to set things on fire, overreacts and sees danger where there is none.

Lachesis 30c, For anger that results in physical afflictions, aversions to tight or constrictive clothing especially around the neck, jealousy, fear of snakes, temper tantrums, violence and anger from frustration.

Causticum 30c, For anger at injustice, desire to fight for others, activist tendencies, does not follow rules, indignation, irritability, weakness and restlessness, coughing, bed wetting, arthritis and great sensitivity to the suffering of strangers in the news.

Sulphur 30c, For the person who is untidy and averse to bathing, fearless and with an intellectual disposition. Other symptoms include itchy skin problems, hot feet and night, impatience and angry outbursts when frustrated, thwarted, victimized or dealt with unjustly but cools off quickly.  The person feels better from open air and worse from heat.
Oxygen 30c, For the person who is demanding, complains loudly and feels victimized and indignant but does not do anything to improve the situation.
Cimicifuga 30c, 2D,  OR Arnica 6c, 1D

remedies.condition: Anxiety, Worry - Anxiety and irrational worries can persist after a traumatic event due to an undermined sense of safety, stability and control.  When coping skills are overwhelmed, and the symptoms of anxiety linger after disaster has passed or are out of proportion to the situation, homeopaths recommend:
remedies: [BP] For Emotional Shock, grief, anxiety, depression: Ignatia 200c, 2D

Aconitum 30c, For severe anxiety, agitation or terror and fear of death, extreme restlessness and tossing about, aversion to light, red eyes,hot,red, flushed and swollen face, becoming very pale and faint upon rising, chest pain, convulsions, violet palpitation of the heart, buzzing in the ears,throbbing headache and great thirst.

Argentum nitricum 30c, For dizziness, apprehension and anticipatory anxiety before an even such as a job interview or first date, cravings for sweets and salt, imagining all kinds of calamities in the future, feeling hurried, feeling suffocated with anxiety, making mistakes from nervousness, self-destructive thoughts or impulses, obsessive compulsive superstitions, fear of heights, flying and crowds, claustrophobia, diarrhea and fear of failure and of losing control.  The person feels worse from heat and better from cool, fresh air.

Arsenicum album 30c, For worries about health, fastidiousness, obsession about small details, need to control everything, anxiety about the future and his own safety, perfectionism, critical, chilly, desire for company, restlessness despite exhaustion, digestive troubles or asthma attacks and thirst for frequent small drinks.

Calcarea carbonica 30c, For the person who fears change and tires easily.  Other symptoms include perspiration and night sweats, frequent chilliness, inability to stay warm, muddled thinking when tired, which makes the person feel anxious and fear losing his mind, great hunger, craving for eggs and slow digestion.

Cocculus 30c, For insomnia and exhaustion due to worry about others, a feeling that everything is moving in slow motion, feelings of unreality, confusion and dizziness.  The person does not realize that he is tired while he is caring for others and does not allow himself to relax.

Gelsemium 30c, For chills and hot flashes, stage fright apprehension and anxiety before an event, with weakness, trembling and a desire to hide.  The person is paralyzed with fear and unable to cope.  Other symptoms include diarrhea, fatigue and achiness, a heavy feeling and drooping eyelids.
Kali arsenicosum 30c, For anxiety about heart problems.  The anxiety is worse at night.

Kali phosphoricum 30c, For the person who is overwhelmed and feels unable to cope and who is overly sensitive,skittish and easily startled.  The person has difficulty concentrating and experiences insomnia and backache.  The person feels better from rest, warmth and a meal.

Lycopodium clavatum 30c, For mental stress and fear of failure, lack of self-confidence, belching,flatulence and constipation or diarrhea, irritability and crankiness and cravings for sweets. Theperson may be boasting or bragging and bullying but is meek and intimidated with strangers or those who have power.  The Person has an aversion to company.

Phosphorus 30c, For the person who is nervous, excitable and easily startled with an overactive imagination and intense and vivid fears. Additional symptoms include great sensitivity, night sweats, and exhausted, spacey feeling,heart palpitations, tight chest, hoarse voice, desire for company and reassurance, craving for cold foods and drinks.  The person feels better from concussion and talking, is sympathetic and cannot tolerate others’ suffering.  Theperson will ask for pain medication for others because he cannot stand to see them in pain.
Manganum 30c, For apprehensiveness, anxiety and fear.  The more the person tries to distract himself,the more anxious he gets until it is impossible to think.  The person feels better lying down.

Mercurius solubilis 30c, For anxiety that arises after suffering some kind of abuse or trauma, including fears that it will happen again, feeling of being on edge or “walking on eggshells,” expecting something bad to happen, fears about saying or doing the wrong thing, fear of being alone, of the dark, of being followed, stammering, convulsions or tremors and intense physical pains with sudden onset.

Natrum muriaticum 30c  For The person who experiences anxiety after having been betrayed, is afraid to trust again and become closed and controlled.  The person who is hypersensitive and easily hurt, brooding, with anxiety about health and diet, germs and hygiene, anxiety about intruders at night, perfectionism, compassion for others, self-consciousness with extreme modesty or bashfulness, insomnia, chest pain and tightness and migraines.

Nux vomica 30c, For the person who feels he is running out of time or that time is moving too slowly or too quickly and he can’t get things done.  This person worries about wasting time,is nervous in anticipation of other’s disappointment with him,worries about making a mistake that will result in time wasted orisparalyzed by anxiety.  The person tends to be a workaholic that must keep busy and productive,is obsessive compulsive or experience cramping and spasms, constipation, tightness in the chest and asthma.

Pulsatilla 30c, For the person who is insecure and experiences anxiety with clinginess and moodiness,has a great need for attention and comforting and an aversion to being alone and is easily discouraged.  The person feels worse in warm stuffy rooms and feels better in the open air.  Other symptoms include lack of thirst, insomnia with repetitive thoughts and worries about others.

Silicea 30c, For the person who is nervous, shy, serious and occasionally lacking in confidence and experiences anxiety when given a new task or when evaluated.  Other symptoms include headaches, difficulty concentrating, exhaustion and oversensitivity from overwork, accompanied by dread,overreacting,lack of stamina and indecision over trifles as well as important things.

Belladonna: early stages after Iris Tenax has failed. Look for headache, increased temperature without perspiration, restlessness, vomiting, extreme abdominal tenderness with a red hot face. The person feels better lying on their back in the knees-to-chest position
Mercurius: For hard, hot, painful swelling in lower right quadrant of the abdomen. You may be able to feel a tender lump in the area of the appendix.

Bryonia: For throbbing or sharp pain confined to limited spot in the abdomen that is relieved by absolute rest or by lying on teh painful side. This person lies perfectly still with the knees drawn up in the fetal position. Constipation accompanies this picture.
Natrum sulphuricum - for pain in lower right side that moves around to the back, accompanied by jaundice.
Dioscorea: For constant relentless twisting and gripping pain, with a great deal of gas in the intestine. The pain lessens with bending backward.
Rhus tox: extensive abdominal swelling and pain that leads to restlessness.
Arsenicum album: For chills, diarrhea, and restlessness. Arsenicum has also been used to relieve vomiting in these circumstances. This is an excellent remedy when sepsis is indicated.

Lachesis: For pain that from lower right back of the abdomen back and down toward the thighs. The person prefers to lie on his back with knees drawn up. Lachesis has been used to reduce appendicitis pain when the pain is aggravated by the slightest touch.
Pyrogenium: For peritonitis and sepsis after appendix bursts.
Colocynthis: For intense, cutting, cramping or twisting pain in lower right quadrant of the abdomen. This pain is relieved by heat and pressure.
Ignatia: The key symptom is fear of appendectomy. The body is hot and rigid with extreme pain in the region of the appendix. The person is very sensitive and alert with great anxiety.

Only female spider bites are toxic.  Signs of a Black Widow bite include numbness and pain around the bite site, painful cramping, rigidity of the abdominal muscles, chest tightness and difficulty breathing, dizziness, sweating, nausea and vomiting. Traditional treatment includes antivenom, calcium gluconate injection, and intravenous Robaxin.
[It is possible to save the spider and make a remedy]`

The bitten area will not be painful until hours later.  The site eventually develops a bluish color and then putrefies, forming an ulcer or crater of dead tissue.  A serious systemic reaction may occur with fever, chills, joint pain and vomiting.  There is no antivenom, and the affected tissue must be surgically removed. There is great danger of sepsis/tetanus

If the snake is dead, keep the body.  If the snake is alive, and you can observe it safely, take note of what kind of snake it is.  The head is the most important part for species identification. Venemous snake present symptoms within an hour.
The bitten person should lie down with the injured part immobilized

remedies.condition: Bites, Crotalidae/Pit Viper Snakes - (Rattlesnakes, moccasins, copperhead, bushmaster, fer-de-lance, habu, Russell’s viper, etc.)
remedies: Symptoms include a burning sensation at the puncture site, swelling and skin discoloration, weakness, sweating, blood in the urine, severe headache and thirst and fainting a few hours after the bite.  Death may occur within 24-48 hours if untreated.  Keep the bitten area below the heart level.  Treat for shock. Antivenom should be administered within 1 hour.  Be prepared to treat for anaphylactic reaction to antivenom if it occurs.  If no antivenom is available, then the incision and suction method should be used within 1 hour.  Make an incision through the puncture sites along the direction of the muscle.  The cut should be ⅛ inch to ¼ inch deep.  Suction using a mechanical device or the mouth if free from cavities, cuts and sores.

remedies.condition: Bites, Crotalidae family snakes have substances in their venom that cause thrombosis, gangrene and leakage of capillary blood into the tissue. Venomous snake bites will present symptoms within an hour.
remedies: DO NOT make an “X” cut, DO NOT cut into joints or tendons, DO NOT apply a tourniquet or constricting band, DO NOT ice the wound, DO NOT allow the person to eat or drink any alcohol
Lachesis 30c
Crotalus horridus 30c
Pyrogenium 30c, 48 hours after the bite to prevent sepsis

remedies.condition: Bites, Elapidae/Coral Snakes   -          (Also Kraits, cobras, mambas, asps, etc.)
remedies: There are few immediate physical symptoms, but a venomous bite will affect behavior after a few hours.  This will be followed by eye paralysis and difficulty breathing, severe headache, dizziness and lack of coordination, weakness and lethargy, muscular twitching, nausea, vomiting, and cardiac arrest.

remedies.condition: Remember “red on yellow, dangerous fellow.” The coral snake is small with red, yellow and black bands, with the red bordering the yellow.
remedies: Apply a tourniquet immediately over a bone near the bite, for example, if the bite is on the shin, apply the tourniquet above the knee.  This should be loosened every 20c, minutes for 30c, seconds to allow fresh blood into the area.  Administer antivenom if available.  DO NOT use morphine or any drugs that slow respiration.  Incision and suction should not be attempted, as it will not help with these types of snake bites.
Lachesis 30c
Elaps corallines 30c, (coral snake venom)
Crotalus horridus 30c, if bleeding persists.

For any bite, flush 3 times daily with 1 tsp of Calendula or hypericum tincture in a quart of water (1:10). If the bite wound is more like a puncture wound, use Hypericum; if it is an open wound, with torn flesh, use Calendula. Human bites that break the skin are just as dangerous as animal bites due to the bacteria in human mouths.  Any bite wound should be monitored for signs of infection.  Avoid closing or cauterizing the wound initially, so as not to trap infectious agents inside.

Arsenicum 30c, for infected bites that are dark, black, unhealthy looking and gangrenous.  The person exhibits restlessness, fear and anxiety.  If edematous swelling use Apis 200c.

Belladonna 30c, for rapid swelling and violent symptoms, including redness, heat, throbbing and burning and red streaks spreading out from the sting.  Other symptoms include flushed face, dilated pupils, glassy eyes, fever, mental excitement; and delirium.
Caladium 30c, for those who seem to attract biting flies, helps to prevent bites, especially intensely burning and stinging small insect bites such as those of fleas, blackflies, or mosquitos.
Hypericum 30c, for shooting pain following the nerves upward, tingling, burning and numbness, bites or stings on the fingers or toes.  Symptoms become worse from cold.
Ledum 30c, for puncture wounds, especially if the bitten area feels cold but the person does not want the part covered or heated.  The person feels cold but feels better from cold applications.
Natrum Mur 30c, is used after Apis when symptoms linger and for bites that look like hives.
Urtica Urens 30c, for redness, blotchiness, burning and itching like that caused by stinging nettles and for bee stings to the face.
Staphysagria 30c, is used as a preventative against biting insects and is reputed to act as a repellent.  Take one dose before going into an area where likely to be bitten.

remedies.condition: Blood- Bleeding   - Bright red blood that flows heavily with pulsating or spurting indicates arterial bleeding, which can be very dangerous.  If an artery is cut at angle it will automatically seal itself off within a few minutes, but if it is cut straight across, it will hemorrhage unless pressure is applied to stop the bleeding.    Darker red, slower flowing blood comes from a vein.  To slow bleeding, apply direct pressure to the wound site using a sterile dressing, towel, clothing, or your hand.  If the dressing becomes soaked with blood, put a new dressing or towel on top without removing it.  The wound should not be disturbed.
remedies: Arnica Montana 30c, for arterial bleeding
Phosphorus 30c, if there is no improvement after Arnica
Hamamelis Virginiana 30c, for venous or capillary bleeding
Crotalus Horridus 30c, This remedy is generally used when there is bleeding around the heart and/or generalized bleeding from several different areas, such as the eyes, nose, mouth.

To slow bleeding, apply direct pressure to the wound site using a sterile dressing, towel, clothing, or your hand.  If the dressing becomes soaked with blood, put a new dressing or towel on top without removing it.  The wound should not be disturbed.

If the bleeding is not stopping, elevate wound above heart level. If none of the above slows down the bleeding, then pressure should be applied at one of the pressure points. Choose pressure point closest to the wound that lies between the wound and the heart where you can feel the pulse.

Pressure applied to one of these points for more than 10 min. Can result in necrosis of limb below the pressure point or possible renal failure if toxins build up below the pressure point and then are released back into the circulating blood stream.

SEE ALSO EPISTAXIS.
remedies: Phosphorus 30c, 1 or 2 doses, will usually stop a nosebleed within 5 mins. This is a common remedy for nose bleeds, especially if blood is bright red and person is prone to nosebleeds.

remedies.condition: Boils  -   Choose one and administer 2D                                  Boils: Boils are hard, inflamed, swollen tissue, usually round and often under the skin.  They can be painful, throbbing and red.  Treat with hot compresses and soking in very warm water and cover with a clean dressing.
remedies: Arnica Montana - For a tendency to small boils that are painful. When one boil is healed, another one comes out or the boils occur in crops. The skin is black and blue, itching and burning. This remedy is also used for eruptions of small pimples.
Arsenicum Album 30c, - For severe infection, burning pain that is relieved by warmth, offensive discharge, exhaustion, anxiousness and restlessness.

Belladonna 30c, - For the early stages of a boil when it feels painful and hot but shows little or no pus yet. Inflammation makes the skin shiny with stabbing pain at the infection site. The person may be feverish or agitated.
Bellis Perennis - For boils all over the body, with soreness, pain and marked swelling.
Calcarea Carb - For extremely painful boils that are pale, not red.
Carcarea Picrata - For chronic and recurring boils.
Carbolic Acid - For painful burning boils with an accumulation of pus and discharge with a very foul odor.

Hepar Sulph 30c - For boils that are extremely tender and sensitive with sharp sticking pain as if a splinter is lodged in the skin. These boils have deep pockets of pus, are sensitive to cold and are slow to heal even after draining. The person is touchy and feels vulnerable.
Ledum Pal - For boils and felons caused by the prick of a needle.
Lachesis 30c - For boils that are bluish purple in color, with dark, thick pus.

Merc Iod - An excellent remedy for the inflammatory stage of boils, when there is no pus drainage yet, and the skin is an intense shiny red color with throbbing and pain. When the pus forms, it is green, thin and watery.

Mercurius Sol 30c - The first choice after pus has formed. This type of boil will not be as sensitive as a boil requiring Hepar Sulph, but it is aggravated by warmth and changes in temperature. For large, persistent boils. The skin is moist or greasy looking with swollen lymph nodes and bad breath.

Silicea 30c - Use when a boil is slow to come to a head, slow to heal (even when draining) and when many form at once. This remedy is especially used for recurring boils, especially along the edges of the nostrils and the external ear. Warm compresses are soothing. The person is easily fatigued and suffers from frequent colds and swollen glands. Firm red lumps that remain even after the boil has healed often disappear when the person takes Silicea.

Sulphur 30c - Use when painful, sensitive boils appear repeatedly, especially in crops. The person is sensitive to cold and may be feverish and irritable. Application of heat makes the boil feel better.
Tarentula Cubensis 30c - Boils with acute, severe pain (stinging and throbbing). The skin has a purple or bluish discoloration. The person is restless and has difficulty sleeping.
Thuja - For groups of boils on the face or neck.
Tuberculinum - When crops of small intensely painful boils successively appear on the nose.
Hepar Sulph - For emerging boils with yellow pus that smells bad. It will absorb a boil early on before it comes to a head. Once the boil opens, use Silicea.
Echinacea Angustifolia MT - To support the immune system while fighting an infection, place 7 drops in ¼ glass of water. Administer 3 times per day for an adult.
Treat with hot compresses and soaking in very warm water and cover with a clean dressing.

remedies.condition: Bones  - Fracture, Broken Bones -    If there is any deformity of the injured limb, then a fracture is likely.  There will be swelling and localized pain.  A sprain or strained muscle, on the other hand, will show pain that is spread out over a larger area.  If a fracture is suspected, DO NOT press on the area, as this can cause greater damage and induce deeper shock to the system. Fractures to the skull or spine are the most serious.  Do not move the victim more than necessary.
remedies: [If one is not sure whether a bone is broken or not, ONE single dose of Symphytum 30c, may be administered.  If it relieves the pain, then the bone is broken, but if it does not relieve the pain, the bone is not broken.]
Arnica Montana 30c, Administer the first dose, then splint the limb above and below the fracture until someone can set the bone
Aconitum Napellus 30c, if still suffering from shock
Hypericum Perforatum 30c, If there is accompanying nerve pain

Symphytum 30c, AFTER the bone has been set, speeds up bone healing.  It does this so well that this remedy should not be administered before the broken bone is set, as this can result in the bones starting to knit together in the wrong position, 1 dose daily until significant relief is achieved.
Ruta Graveolens 30c, when there are muscle or tendon injuries as well as multiple fractures.
Bellis Perennis 30c, 1 dose a week after injury

remedies.condition: Breathing - Shortness of Breath (Dyspnea, Tachypnea)                                                                                                                                                                          Administer 1 dose every 5-15 minutes as needed based upon severity.  As a person's symptoms diminish in intensity, reduce the frequency of dosage to once every 1-4 hours.
remedies: [BP] For dyspnea: Carbo Vegetabilis 200c, or Lachesis 200
Aconitum 30c, This remedy is useful in early stage of acute attacks, with marked anxiety, fear and restlessness.

Arnica 30c, For dyspnea due to traumatic injury.  The person answers questions correctly despite seeming unconscious, is averse to touch and assistance, says there is nothing wrong and wants to be left alone.

Arsenicum album 30c, For dyspnea, cyanosis, anxiety, and restlessness, fastidious and exacting, dyspnea from respiratory infection, profuse cold sweat, chills and wheezing.  The person feels worse from lying down, laughing, climbing stairs, odors, wet weather and cold and feels better from warmth, coffee and from sitting up or walking around.

Kali carbonicum 30c, For dyspnea alternating with vertigo or diarrhea, weakness, irritability and contrariness, stabbing pains that are worse from 2 am to early morning and from any motion, peevishness and irritability and anxiety but not as severe as found with Arsenicum. The person feels better from leaning forward with the elbows on the knees.

Lycopodium clavatum 30c, For dyspnea from humiliation or embarrassment, shallow, short breaths with a rattling sound and chest tightness with burning pain.  The person feels worse from lying on the back, taking deep breaths and cold air and feels better from warm drinks.  This person wants fresh air but complains of cold.

Lobelia inflata 30c, For panting dyspnea preceded by prickling pains all over the body, gastric troubles with gas, nausea or vomiting, chest constriction, panic and fear of death, dry mouth and throat, mouth breathing, lump in the throat above the sternum, hyperventilation that is worse after exertion, tobacco smoke and cold.

Lachesis 30c, For dyspnea that occurs during sleep or on rising, nervous cough, purplish skin, inability to bear tight or constrictive clothing, exhaustion in the morning and symptoms that move from left to right.  The person is high strung, jealous and very talkative and feels worse from pressure on the neck or chest.

Carbo vegetabilis 30c, For blue skin, cough, hollow sensation and burning pain in the chest, cold breath and skin, air hunger, the desire to be fanned, bloated abdomen, rapid breathing, anxiety, chills, weakness or faintness and coughing that leads to gagging.  The person feels better from sitting up and burping and feels worse from talking, eating, lying down and in the evening.

remedies.condition: Breathing - Shortness of Breath (Dyspnea, Tachypnea)                                                                                                                                                                          Administer 1 dose every 5-15 minutes as needed based upon severity.  As a person's symptoms diminish in intensity, reduce the frequency of dosage to once every 1-4 hours.
remedies: Cactus grandiflorus 30c, For rattling breaths, pressure in the chest, pulmonary or cardiac problems, constricting pains with a feeling of an iron band constricting the chest, spasmodic pains, anxiety and sadness, numbness and tingling of the extremities, restless legs and insomnia.  The person feels better from open air and feels worse from walking, ascending stairs or lying on the left side.

Phosphorus 30c, For wheezing cough, a feeling of heat and dryness in the chest, dyspnea from exhaustion, atmospheric or temperature changes, trembling, cough that causes abdominal pain and a desire for company and cold drinks.  The person feels worse from lying on the left side, in the morning, at twilight or in the evening and feels better from lying on the right side.

Natrum Sulph 30c, For dyspnea in damp weather, extreme sensitivity to moisture, holding the chest while coughing, wheezing aggravated by exertion and morning diarrhea.  The person feels worse in the early morning.

Naja tripudians 30c, For the person who feels as if there is a weight on the chest like a burning hot spike piercing the chest.  The person feels worse lying down and at night and better from sitting up.

Ipecacuanha 30c, For gasping breathlessness and spasmodic cough, severe nausea unrelieved by vomiting, violent incessant cough with redness or blueness of face alternating with paleness, thirstlessness, wheezing, gagging, retching, a feeling of heaviness in the chest, profuse perspiration with clammy skin and a desire for fresh air.  The person feels worse from motion and warmth and feels better from rocking back and forth.

Antimonium tart 30c, For severe respiratory distress, bluish skin, wet rattling breaths and cough that is worse in the early morning and from lying down, irritability, drowsiness, fluid in the lungs, thickly coated tongue, thirstlessness, severe nausea that is better from vomiting or lying on the right side, extreme fatigue, dyspnea caused by an infection such as bronchitis or obstructive pulmonary disease and a desire to be left alone.

Nux vomica 30c, For dyspnea accompanied by digestive problems, irritability, impatience, hypersensitivity, spasms, feeling as though one must pass stools during the attack but cannot, yellow coated tongue, constricted chest and fullness, heaviness and pressure in the stomach.  The person feels worse in the morning, from excesses of stimulants, alcohol or spicy food and from exertion and feels better from warmth and sleep, belching and chilliness.

Pulsatilla 30c, For dyspnea and wheezing from a warm or stuffy room or from rich food, tightness in the chest, cough with greenish discharge and constriction in the throat or windpipe.  The person feels worse in the evening, at night and from lying down, which makes the person feel smothered, and feels better from cool fresh air and from sitting up.

Spongia tosta 30c, For a barking cough with dyspnea, breathing with a sawing sound, gasping breaths, very hoarse voice or laryngitis and feeling as though breathing through a dry sponge.  The dyspnea comes on while sleeping, with attacks accompanied by terror and fear of death, anxiety and hot flashes and great difficulty inhaling.  The person feels better from warm food or drinks, from sitting up and from leaning either backward or forward and feels better from warm food or drinks, from sitting up and from leaning either backward or forward and feels worse at night, during a full moon, from tobacco smoke, cold drinks and cold dry wind and in the early part of the night.

remedies.condition: Breathing - Shortness of Breath (Dyspnea, Tachypnea) Administer 1 dose every 5-15 minutes as needed based upon severity. As a person's symptoms diminish in intensity, reduce the frequency of dosage to once every 1-4 hours.y
remedies: Apis mellifica 30c, For dyspnea during an acute allergic reaction, usually accompanied by burning stinging pains in the chest , the throat closing up, a tight chest, severe air hunger with panting, a beaten and bruised feeling in the chest, sweetish expectoration and painful breathing with dry wheezing.  The person feels better in open air and feels worse from heat.

Bryonia alba 30c, For dry mucous membranes, great thirst, dyspnea made worse from every movement, a desire to breathe deeply but cannot bear to take a deep breath from pain, stitching pains in the chest, a desire to be left alone, accelerated breathing, weakness and apathy.

Sambucus nigra 30c, For sudden attacks during the night, bluish skin and gasping breaths, severe anxiety, dry blocked nasal passages, icy cold hands and feet but the person says they feel hot, profuse perspiration, frightening attacks throughout the night alternating with sleep and dry skin with a burning sensation.  The dyspnea is more severe than with ipecacuanha but with a less severe cough.  Breathing is obstructed when lying down, but the person feels slightly better from sitting up.

Cuprum metallicum 30c, For nausea, spasmodic complaints, severe dyspnea and cyanosis, dyspnea alternating with vomiting, violent cough causing breathlessness, gurgling cough, convulsions, trembling, cramps, hiccups, a head that feels empty or as though someone poured water over it and suffocative attacks around 3 am. The person feels worse from coughing, vomiting, bending backward and breathing in cold air and feels better from cold drinks.

Stannum metallicum 30c, For dyspnea from exhaustion and the least effort, severe weakness, a need to loosen clothes, a raw, hollow feeling in the chest and profuse sweetish expectation.  The person feels worse from talking.
Thuja 30c, For dyspnea following vaccination, a red face, easy expectoration and short difficult breaths.  The person feels worse from taking a deep breath or talking, from cold weather and at night.

Sanguinaria canadensis 30c, For irritability and anger, hot palms and soles of the feet, humming or roaring in the ears, painful sensitivity to noise, distended temporal veins, chest constriction, sharp, stitching pains in the chest, headache, aversion to butter and intense thirst.  The person feels worse from sweets and feels better from lying down.

Chelidonium majus 30c, For jaundiced skin, pain below the right scapula, lethargy, disinclination to make any effort, insecurity, uncertainty, vertigo in which objects seem to turn in a circle, accelerated breathing, difficulty breathing, pain when taking deep breaths, pain in the right side of the chest and shoulder and constricted chest.  The person feels better from eating and from pressure and feels worse from motion, touch and changes in weather.

Sulphur 30c, For burning in the chest, a heart that feels too large, a feeling of a load on the chest, desire to open windows, stitching pains made worse by taking a deep breath or lying on the back, difficulty breathing after talking, feeling hot and smelly, itchy skin, thirst for ice cold drinks and indigestion with burning.  The person feels better from warm drinks and has a large appetite.  Missing meals causes stomachache or irritability.

remedies.condition: Breathing - Hyperventilation      Hyperventilation refers to breathing that is deeper and faster than normal.  It is usually triggered by severe acute anxiety, although it can also be caused by injury or illness.  The resulting decrease of carbon dioxide in the blood causes lightheadedness, rapid heartbeat and numbness or tingling of extremities and can result in loss of consciousness.  Panic and hyperventilation can create a vicious cycle, as the physical effects of hyperventilation lead to greater anxiety, especially the feeling of suffocation.
remedies: Aconite 30c, For sudden onset, hyperventilation due to shock, injury, illness or anxiety, restlessness and fear of death, heart palpitations, numbness and a feeling of pins and needles.  The person feels better from lying on his back.

Ignatia 30c, For use after loss or disappointment.  Symptoms include sighing, yawning, a feeling of weight or constriction in the chest, erratic mood swings, a lump in the throat, changeable symptoms, cramps and spasms, hypochondria, twitching facial muscles, headache and cravings for acidic food or drinks.  The person feels worse from tobacco or coffee and feels better while eating or from changing position.

Nux moschata 30c, For the person who is sleepy, absent-minded or giddy, with heaviness and oppression of the chest, a feeling in the throat of being strangled, dry mucous membranes, dry eyes, constipation, hypochondria and fainting at the sight of blood.

Argentum nitricum 30c, For restlessness, claustrophobia, fear of heights, feeling anxious and always too rushed, diarrhea from being anxious, panic attacks on planes and bridges and fear in narrow places.
Asafoetida 30c, For hypochondria, irritability, bad mood, spasmodic air hunger, lungs that do not expand sufficiently, distended abdomen and good appetite with a craving for wine.

Lobelia inflata 30c, For constriction in the chest, air hunger, rattling sound when breathing, anger and irritability, a faint feeling in the pit of the stomach, constant salivation, spasms in the esophagus, nausea, sweating, prickling of the skin, a sensation as if the heart is standing still and intense hypochondria about chest and heart complaints.  The person feels better from walking in fresh air, warmth, a small meal and beer and feels worse from fatigue, exertion, eating, drafts and from bathing in cold water.

Phosphorus 30c, For the person who is restless, over excited, easily frightened, very suggestible, sociable and craves company.  This person is alert and interested in the surroundings.  Other symptoms include coughs and digestive complaints, bright red bleeding, a desire for large amounts of cold drinks, vomiting and diarrhea.

Nux vomica 30c, For impatience, irritability, spasmodic tightening of the chest, cramps and twitching and sensitivity to and irritation from lights, odors, noises and alcohol.  The person feels better from heat and warm drinks.

China officinalis 30c, For intense nervous sensitivity, exhaustion from fluid loss, puffing and blowing air out of the cheeks and a good appetite but food sits like a rock in the stomach.  The person is sensitive to and irritated by any touch, but firm pressure relieves.  The person feels better from raising the head up.
Kali carbonicum 30c, For pain in the chest when speaking, fear of ghosts and of being left alone.  The person startles easily and feels worse from cold and drafts.

Ammonium carbonicum 30c, For great weakness and lethargy, lack of endurance, hyperventilation after walking or ascending stairs or hills, heart palpitations, irritability, gloominess, agitation and annoyance about things others have done to offend the person, aversion to cold air and bathing and sensitivity of the teeth and gums in which clenching the teeth causes shocks of pain throughout the head.

Moschus 30c, For the person who is nervous and icy cold, with one cheek cold and red while the other is pale and warm. Other symptoms include a sensation of falling from a height, vertigo, fainting and palpitation, nervousness and tendency to drop things from hurry, tight constricted chest and a smothering sensation.  The person feels better from warmth and feels worse from cold.

remedies.condition: Breathing - Asphyxia, Suffocation, Drowning, Hanging or Strangulation     When respiration ceases, death or irreversible brain damage usually results within a few minutes.  Suffocation can be caused by blocked airways, such as a foreign body that causes choking, or compression of the airways such as in strangulation, an environmental lack of oxygen such as drowning in fluid or being wrapped in a small space without ventilation or failure of the body to take in oxygen due to illness or injury to the lungs, heart or brain.  The first course of action when someone is not breathing is to check the airways and perform some sort of artificial respiration such as CPR or mouth-to-mouth resuscitation.      Signs of suffocation include swollen veins in the neck, blue lips, fingers and toes, fast weak pulse, loss of consciousness and frothing at the mouth or nostrils.  After breathing has stopped, the heart may continue to beat for another 10 minutes during which time the life can still be saved by artificial respiration.  If the person is not breathing, unconscious or in a coma, give Opium 30c, or Carbo Vegetabilis 30c, 1 dose in aqueous solution, rubbed on the underside of the wrist.
remedies: Aconitum 30c, For panic and fear of death, restlessness and tossing about, red eyes, photophobia and a hot, red, flushed, swollen face that becomes deathly pale on rising.

Opium 30c, For the person who is unconscious or semi-conscious, with hot, red, bloated, swollen dark face. Other symptoms include spasmodic twitching, especially at the corners of the mouth with the lower jaw hanging down, bloodshot and half-closed eyes with pupils that are contracted and unresponsive, painlessness and sluggishness. This remedy is used for stroke or for those who have been strangled or hanged.

Carbo Vegetabilis 30c, The homeopathic ‘corpse reviver’ is used for near-death states, when the person is in a state of collapse and is almost gone. Highlights include a barely perceptible pulse, exhaustion with great weakness, chilliness, cold sweat, gasping for air, hot headedness, chilliness, cold sweat, gasping for air, hot head with cold extremities, belching, flatulence and distension, excessive saliva, heart failure, a puffy, bluish, cold face, blue or mottled skin, twitching of the upper lip, black swollen tongue coated with yellow-brown, rattling in the throat, pupils that do not react to light, fainting after rising from sleep and while still in bed, fainting from dehydration, heart palpitations and sluggishness. If the person is bleeding, the blood is dark red and oozing. If the person is conscious, he may want to be fanned or want the windows open. This remedy is used for Carbon monoxide or coal gas poisoning.

Laurocerasus 30c, For breathlessness, collapse, faintness, lack of reaction, gasping or suffocating that is better from lying down, long lasting faints, symptoms brought on by fright, heat and/or respiratory failure, chilliness unhelped by warmth, blue or mottled skin, slow, feeble pulse and an appearance of being poisoned.  The person regains color very slowly.  This remedy is used for strangulation from a wrapped umbilical cord.  The person feels worse from exertion and from sitting up, which causes gasping.

Antimonium tart 30c, For drowning or fluid in the lungs, lack of pulse, apparent death, rapid, short, difficult breathing, rattling of the chest, objects caught in the throat, mucus in the airway, cold, blue, pale face covered with cold sweat, sunken face and eyes, dilated flaring nostrils, shriveled lips, trembling of chin and lower jaw, drowsiness, chills, nausea, vomiting and a desire for frequent small sips of water.  This remedy is used for newborns who do not breathe.

Arnica montana 30c, For physical injury or trauma.  The person has a sunken face with droopy eyes and is seemingly unconscious but answers questions coherently and then relapses.  The person has an aversion to touch and company and asks to be left alone.  This remedy is also for people who say they are fine when they are obviously injured and in shock.

Camphora 30c, For collapse with icy coldness of the entire body, lack of reaction, weak pulse, haggard red or bluish distorted face with cold sweat, fixed, staring eyes with dilated pupils, photophobia if conscious and objects look bright and glittering.  The person has an aversion to being covered, becomes pale and faint upon rising, feels better from thinking about the symptoms and feels worse from motion.

China 30c, For apparent death from bleeding.  Symptoms include sallow, bloodless, pale, bloated face, weakness and decreased levels of consciousness, dehydration from loss of fluids, dizziness when walking, indifference, apathy and despondence.

remedies.condition: Breathing - Asphyxia, Suffocation, Drowning, Hanging or Strangulation Continued
remedies: Lachesis 30c, For apparent death when the person is not breathing and has hardly any pulse.  Highlights include suffocation from anything that comes near the mouth or nose, vertigo, pale face, nausea, vomiting, stitching pains around the heart, cold sweat, hot skin, spasms, trembling tongue, stiffening and swelling.

Coffea 30c, For fits of suffocation, aversion to open air, twitching, shivering and convulsions, short inspirations with heaving chest; sore throat that feels worse when swallowing, red cheeks and hot face, lively red eyes, clear sight, hearing that is more acute than normal, great sensitivity to noises and great sensitivity to touch or contact.

Ipecacuanha 30c, For foreign objects in the windpipe, dry rattling cough, weak lungs from bleeding, constant constriction in chest, cramps, spasms, convulsions, violent coughing with every breath, suffocation from mucus, severe dyspnea with wheezing and anxiety, cold sweat and lack of thirst.

Phosphorus 30c, For fluid in the lungs, restlessness and over excitability, coughs and digestive complaints, bright red bleeding, a desire for large amounts of cold drinks, vomiting and diarrhea, wheezing, a feeling of heat and dryness in the chest, trembling and a cough that causes abdominal pain.  The person desires cold drinks, is very suggestible and easily frightened and is sociable and craves company.  The person feels worse from lying on the left side, in the morning, at twilight or in the evening and feels better from lying on the right side.
See: Smoke

remedies.condition: First Degree: The outer layer of the skin is partially burned. The skin turns red but does not blister, with some swelling and pain.
remedies: Urtica Urens 30c- for first degree burns, where the burn has a prickly or stinging sensation. One dose 4 times a day.
Arnica 30c- To reduce pain and swelling at the burn site
Kali mur 6x and Ferr phos 6x- These cell salts help calm inflammation in combination with the other remedies listed above.

Topical Treatments: Coconut oil, unsalted butter or calendula salve/ointment applied to the burn will speed healing. Diluted Urtica Urens tincture can be applied topically to the burn as well. Soak a gauze pad in a diluted tincture (one teaspoon of tincture in a pint of water) and apply it to the burned area. Add a few drops of the diluted tincture to the gauze when it dries out.

remedies.condition: Second Degree: The first layer of the skin and part of the second layer is partially burned. The skin turns red and blisters with severe pain, splotching and pain.
remedies: Utica urens 30c- for first degree burns, where the burn has a prickly or stinging sensation. One dose 4 times a day.
Cantharis 30c - For second degree burns where teh skin has a raw, irritated or blistered appearance and sensation. Administer one dose every 15 minutes.
Arnica 30c- To reduce pain and swelling at the burn site
Acontitum napellus 30c- For shock after the burn
Kali mur 6x and Ferr phos 6x- These cell salts help calm inflammation in combination with the other remedies listed above to help quell the inflammation.

Topical Treatments: Hypericum lotion applied to the burned area can bring relief of pain. Calendula ointment applied around the edge of the burns and over the healing burns in the final stages of recovery speeds the healing process. Alternating Hypericum and Calendula every few days may help if there is a standstill in the healing process. Coconut oil and unsalted butter applied to the burned skin will soothe the area and promote healing.

remedies.condition: Third Degree: All skin layers are burned. The burn may extend to fat and muscle and go all the way to the bone. The flesh may be charred black or it may look dry and white, like wax. For the following remedies, use 30c potency and aminister 1 dose every 15 minutes. Stop when improvement is seen.
remedies: Aconitum 30c- The go-to remedy for shock after a traumatic injury.
Causticum 30c- For teh person who has deep burns that penetrate the flesh.
Hypericum 30c- For relief of extreme nerve pain from deep wounds.
Gloninum 30c- For relief from throbbing pain. Note: Acontium is antidote for Glonoinum, so the two cannot be used together.
Kali Mur 6x and Ferr phos 6x- These cell salts complement homeopathic remedies for burns by reducing inflammation.

Topical Treatment: To promote rapid healing of painful open areas on the skin, apply Calendula tincture to teh affected area in the 1:4 ratio (one part Calendula tincture to 4 parts distilled water). Alternating Hypericum and Calendula every few days may help if there is a standstill in the healing process.
Kali Mur 6X every 30c, minutes until no pain, more often if severe (like every 5 minutes)

[BP]   1st line: (1) Cantharis 200c # Arsenicum Album 200c, e3H.  (2) Cantharis MT (5ml in a cup of water), applied externally, gives instant relief and helps dry up wounds quickly.  Apply two times per day, even in cases where the blisters are open and ulcerated.

2nd line (1) Hepar Sulph Calc 200c + Ars Alb 200c, 4D where suppuration occurs secondary to the burns  (2) Cantharis 200c, 2D     *Sunburn or Scalded mouth: Cantharis 30c.  For burning sensation that is relieved by cold applications, one dose e3h. (more or less frequesntly depending on the severity of the burn)  Take no more than 3 days unless you are sure this is the correct remedy.
Cantharis MT in water applied externally to burns gives relief and helps dry up wounds.
*Belladonna 30c. For red, hot sunburn, sometimes with pulsing sensation
*Ferr phos 6x, Kali mur 6x, Kali Sulph 6x: to accompany any burn remedies above, 1 dose of each, 3-4 times per day, for up to 3 days.
Choose from the following remedies, administer one dose every 15 minutes until improvement is seen.  Increase the time between doses as symptoms improve.
Urtica urens 30c- 1st or 2nd degree burns; prickly or stinging sensation; 1 dose 4xD
Cantharis 30c- 2nd degree; raw, irritated or blistered
Aconite 30c, for shock

Burns, Electrical --An electrical burn may not show much damage on the surface of the skin, but the electricity may have passed deep into the body of the victim. The shock may have caused heart rhythm disturbance or it may have stopped the heart entirely. The victim may have been thrown by the jolt of electricity, and they may have broken bones or other injuries from the fall. If you are helping someone with an electrical burn: 1-Look before you touch! The live wire may still be touching the victim. Touching the victim could pass that current right through you. 2-Turn off the source of electricity if you can. If you cannot turn it off, move the wire (or electrical source) away from you and the victim using a dry, nonconductive object made of cardboard, plastic, or wood. 3- Once the electric current is clearly away from the victim, stop the burning either with water or by smothering the flames with a blanket. 4- Check for signs of life - breathing, coughing or movement. Start CPR if the person is not breathing. 5- Remove any clothing that is smoldering but DO NOT REMOVE anything stuck to the skin. 6- Position the vicim with the head slightly lower than the trunk and the legs elevated and treat for shock. 7- Cover any burned area with a sterile gauze bandage or a clean cloth. Do not use a blanket or towel because loose fibers can stick to the burns. Do not apply any ointments or first aid creams.
For the following remedies, use 30C potency and administer 1 dose every 15 minutes. Stop when improvement is seen
Phosphorus 30c - This is the go-to remedy to reduce pain from electrical burns or electroshock.
Ferr Phos 6X and Nat Sulph 6X - When used along with Phosphorus, these cell salts speed healing.

Opium 30c - Use for the person who is delirious or has become unconscious. The breath comes in rattling gasps, and the upper body is beaded with sweat. Use in aqueous solution and rub the remedy on the inner wrist once every 15 minutes, up to six doses.
Aconitum 30c - For the conscious person in shock after a traumatic injury, 1 dose every 15 minutes.

Burns, Chemical If you need to treat someone for a chemical burn, start by protecting yourself. Put on gloves or an apron to avoid exposing yourself to the chemicals. Flood the burned area with a gentle stream of cool water for at least 20 minutes, making sure the water stays on the burned area, not splashing on another part of the person's body and not splashing onto your body. Remove the person's jewelry or clothing as you are flushing the area with water, not before. Do not remove jewelry or clothing if they are stuck to the person's body. If you know what caused the burn, find the chemical bottle and check for emergency instructions and antidote information on the label. Follow these directions if they are available. Do not put antibiotic ointment, acid or alkali agents on the burn. Once you have finished flushing the burned area with water, wrap the burned area loosely with a dry, sterile dressing.
For the following remedies, use 30C potency and administer 1 dose every 15 minutes. Stop when improvement is seen
Aconitum 30c - For the person in shock after a traumatic injury.
Causticum 30c - The premiere remedy for chemical burns and for deep burns that penetrate into the flesh.
Hepar Sulph 30c - The key to this remedy is blocking the susceptibility to infection. This person's burns are sensitive and painful, and the person may become chilled easily.
Hypericum 30c - For relief of extreme nerve pain from deep wounds.
Glonoinum 30c - For relief from throbbing pain.
Topical treatment: Apply Calendula tincture in a 1:4 ratio (one part Calendula tincture to 4 parts water) to promote rapid healing of painful open areas on the skin.

remedies.condition: Chemical Exposure                        Animals often exhibit symptoms of chemical attack before people do, so watch their behavior if you suspect chemical warfare
remedies: Nerve gas: damages nerves that control bodily functions; progressive loss of control over body; death by asphyxiation
Mustard (blister) type gases: blisters form; attacks mucous membranes; most victims survive
Choking (pulmonary) type gases: attack by irritating the respiratory system, causing suffocation; burning sensation in throat and eyes, cough up white or pink tinged fluid,
Asphyxiant gases: displace oxygen in the blood with another gas; death by asphyxiation
Tear gas & pepper spray: irritants to the eyes and mucous membrane
Cytotoxic Proteins (Ricin): prevent body cells from making protein, cell death results and may lead to death within 36-72 hours
Aconite 30c- initial stages, shock, agitation, fear
Opium 30c-stunned, withdrawn, dazed, unresponsive
Arsenicum album 30c- collapse, weakness, exhaustion, fear

Varicella 30c nosode - As a preventative, especially for those who are immune compromised. One dose weekly.

Rhus Tox 30c - For most of the common chicken pox symptoms, maddening itching, restlessness of mind and body and aching joints, this is generally the only remedy needed. Can be administered prophylactically, 1 dose weekly.

Antimonium Crudum 30c - Symptoms include white-coated tongue, irritability and aversion to being touched or looked at. The skin feels hypersensitive; the touch of clothing, water, etc. is very irritating.

Pulsatilla 30c - For the person who is weepy, in need of loads of attention, wants company, experiences little thirst but is interested in ice cream, peanut butter or creamy cheeses. Each eruption is usually quite large. The itching is not relieved by scratching, and a thick yellow discharge may come from the nose, eyes and ears.

Sulphur 30c - Use when itching is the most prominent feature, the person is feverish or warmer than usual, with odor in the breath, stools, hair or other prominent body odor. Itching is often worse after a bath or shower. The person is not lacking in appetite.
Aconitum Napellus 30c - Use when there is a very sudden violent onset, with restlessness and fear of death.
Antimonium Tartaricum 30c - For cough or bronchial issues with rattling mucus, drowsiness, chills, perspiration and bluish red scabs.
Belladonna 30c - For sudden onset, hot flushed throbbing and burning skin and severe headache. The person is drowsy but cannot sleep.

Mercurius Vivus (or Mercurius Solubilis) 30c - For more severe symptoms, high fevers with profuse sweat and large sore blisters. The person complains of being too hot or too cold and is only comfortable when the temperature is "just right".
Variolinum 30c (smallpox nosode) - For fever, backache, chills, tingling, a feeling as though cold water is trickling down the back, bone pain, headache as though a band is constricting the skull.

Silicea - For overly sensitive, itchy and painful skin, dry, pale skin and mental fatigue, general weakness where the whole body feels sore, always chilly, swollen glands, headaches, smelly feet and lack of vitality.

Natrum Mur - For dry mucus membranes with itchiness. The person feels worse in bed in the morning and worse from consolation but better from open air and music. The person does not want company and experiences a feeling of internal constriction.

remedies.condition: Cholera - Bacterial infection causing severe diarrhea, dehydration; spread through contaminated water
remedies: Cholera:  Cholera is a bacterial infection causing severe diarrhea and dehydration.  Usually spread through contaminated water, it can be fatal in a matter of hours.  Natural disasters, war or any other situation where people live in crowded and poorly sanitized conditions increase the risk of a cholera outbreak.  The cause of death is dehydration, so rehydrating and replenishing electrolytes is the primary treatment.  Cholera can also be contracted from eating raw or undercooked shellfish or exposed to sewage.                                                                                    [BP] 1st Line:     Arsenicum Album 200c, in liquid, 1 dose at beginning of treatment
Veratrum Album 200c + Cuprum Metallicum 6c, 1e2H; to be repeated after every passing of stool and for vomiting
Nat Mur 6X and Kali Phos 6X, e3H to combat dehydration
2nd Line:     Camphora MT or Crude Camphor (one grain) with sugar;  these help immediately in stopping loose stool and vomiting

The favored remedies for the treatment of cholera include the following. Administer 1 dose e15 min, up to 6 doses. As the severity of the symptoms decrease, reduce the frequency. If no improvement is seen after 6 doses, choose a new remedy
Acotinum Napellus 30c - For sudden collapse, anxiety and restlessness, before vomiting or diarrhea sets in.
Arsenicum Album 30c - For burning diarrhea with cold limbs and face. The person desires warmth.
Camphor 30c - For early stages characterized by coldness without sweat, weakness and sudden collapse. The person does not want to be covered despite the coldness.
Carbo Vegetabilis 30c - For ice coldness, bluish skin, collapse from loss of body fluids and difficulty breathing. The person wants fresh air and to be fanned.
Cuprum Metallicum 30c - For use in the later stages with severe cramping or spasms in various parts of the body and cold, blue face. The person feels better from cold drinks and worse from touch.
Veratrum Album 30c - This is the primary remedy for later stages with profuse cold sweat and severe vomiting and diarrhea. The person wants to be covered up and desires warmth.

remedies.condition: Concussion, Head Injury   - A head injury resulting in loss of consciousness always carries risk of brain damage.  Symptoms of concussion include shallow breathing, pale cold skin, nausea and amnesia

Helleborus 30c, 1D.  This remedy is indicated when the person has muscular weakness that progresses toward paralysis.  There may be chewing motions or automatic motions of one arm and leg.
Belladonna 30c, if there is a lot of heat and redness with throbbing pain.  There may be dilated pupils and delirious, frantic behavior.  The victim sees sparks. Light noise aggravate.
Hypericum perforatum 30c, if the concussion causes seizures or spasms.  The person feels worse from touch and better from bending the head backward.
Cicuta virosa 30c, if no better from Hypericum. Violent with moaning and absurd actions.  Complains of chill.  WORSE from drafts and tobacco smoke.
Natrum sulphuricum 30c, if the person has vertigo after the injury or a change in personality, especially suicidal behavior.

Anacardium 30c, - Poison ivy, poison oak, and poison sumac. Useful to reduce swelling and intense itching that is made worse by scratching but relieved by rubbing. Symptoms get worse at night. A warm compress worsens the suffering.

Croton tiglium 30c, - similar to anacardium symptoms, but difference is that it's accompanied by yellowish symptoms, pustules can ooze a yellowish serum, which dries to form thick grayish/brownish scabs. Crot tig is particularly useful when irritation is on the face, genitals, bottocks and lower limbs. Scratching leads to burning and pain. Tends to be left sided and light pressure brings relief. Generally for person that can’t bear the rash.

Graphites 30c, - For a rash that oozes honey-like discharge when it is scratched. Sticky, yellowish and it burns. The skin will have a crusty appearance, especially in the joint areas in the upper arms and legs, and in the torso. Person may dig their nails into the rash, and this rough rubbing, scratching and tearing of the skin brings some relief. Graphites is also indicated for those who feel better when they are wrapped up, as they are generally chilly and feel thick and dull.

Sulphur 30c, - When scratching causes the pustules to bleed, and this bleeding brings relief to the person, look to sulphur. You may see a yellowish or white discharge after scratching, and what remains of the pustule will be crusty and dry. This remedy is indicated when the person feels better in the warmth of a bed but worse after bathing and has an aversion to washing up. Sulphur symptoms are generally left sided, afflict the scalp and may produce facial swelling. The person may be irritable and restless at night.

Rhus Tox 30c, - A hot shower brings relief. Vesicles may line up along a scratch and the skin may actually look angry. The person is restless and irritable. Resting provides no relief, and the symptoms improve by motion and by dry, warm air. Remedy addresses outbreaks on the face, around the eyes, the genitals and on the palms. (Note: because Rhus tox is made from the poison ivy plant, it can be used prophylactically to prevent poison ivy outbreaks. Take a few doses each day for two days.)
Soothing topical remedies -
Baking soda paste: make a paste of baking soda and water and apply to the irritated area. Allow the paste to dry on the skin for relief.
Vinegar compress: Use a half cup of white vinegar in a pint of water. Keep this mixture in the refrigerator and apply it on a cold compress to relieve itching.
Jewelweed: Herbalists recommend crushing fresh jewelweed leaves and applying them to the poison ivy irritation. Jewelweed often grows next to poison ivy.
